node.title: Uber SDE-2 Interview Experience | Offer Received | Take it or try to go senior?

article.title: Uber SDE-2 Interview Experience | Offer Received | Take it or try to go senior?
article.content:
I might be crazy for this given all the layoffs recently, but I have around 6 years of experience and just received an offer from Uber for SDE-2 when I told my rectruiter I'm targeting Senior. I'm not super desparate to switch jobs right now, but want to make the move to the Senior level. It seems like getting promoted internally will be more work than its worth for me at this point, and trying to interview to get to the higher level seems like a better play. Has anyone else upleveled recently to senior while switching jobs?

I'll share my interview experience in case other people are considering Uber soon:

OA:
CodeSignal round that had a couple questions and I was able to get through 2 of them fully. First one was given a graph where each node was a character I had to find all the palindromes. The second one was finding the difference in time between taking the stairs or elevator to go up a building, there were some basic rules about how much time vs energy it takes to go up the stairs vs elevator. You lose energy taking the stairs and gain it by riding the elevator.

Coding 1:
Got asked the alien dictionary problem, [LC 269](https://leetcode.com/problems/alien-dictionary/). This isn't too bad if you're good at topological sort and variations of it come up a lot so it's a good one to practice on anyways.

Coding 2:
I was given a grid with blocked cells, locations of some robots, and a array representing cardinal distances to blockers. The goal was to find robots that were as far apart from blockers in the cardinal directions based on the input array. It seemed like a harder variation of [this problem](https://leetcode.com/discuss/post/7509719/algorithms-data-structures-at-uber-for-l-evqs/) Uber asks. Like the other coding rounds, this one went ok, I was able to get it working and all the test cases passing with a little bit of time left.

Low level design/Machine Coding:
I got asked to design a system to calculate pricing for Uber Eats. Basic requirements were to take into account prices, upgrades, and taxes. The full question is leaked [here](https://offerretriever.com/questions/103). I found this round wasn't too bad, there are a lot of potential followups and I heard Ubers super strict about ensuring that it's fully working, so I was trying to move quickly. There's a good post detailing it [here](https://leetcode.com/discuss/post/7647484/uber-sde-i-interview-experience-feb-2026-snr8/).

System Design:
I think this is the round that sunk it for me to get Senior. I got asked to design a system to visualize the density and availability of drivers in an operational dashboard. This was going ok, until I mentioned that I would use redis as a cache and as a pub sub coordinator to push messages to the dashboard via websockets. My interviewer really dug into the details of how I would set this up in Redis. I felt like I wasn't able to answer this questions well since I haven't actually set something up in Redis for a long time. That messed me up for the rest of the interview and I was second guessing myself a lot more throughout the rest of it.

I think I could do better with another month or so to prep, so I'm debating holding off on settling and trying to go for a senior role at another company. Do you think it's worth holding out in the current job economy, or should I just accept anything that's higher TC and not worse WLB?

post.content: Prep for senior...internal promotion to senior is comparatively more difficult.

If your goal is Senior, taking a bit more time to prep and target the next level makes sense especially since you already know where you stumbled (System Design / Redis). The Uber SDE-2 offer gives you a safety net, but if you’re confident you can clear another round for Senior elsewhere, it could be worth holding out.

Key things to weigh:

Current market — is there enough demand for Senior roles in your stack right now?
TC vs WLB — sometimes a slight downgrade in title is okay if TC or WLB is strong, but your growth trajectory matters more long-term.
Prep time — a month of focused System Design practice could make a big difference.

In short: don’t rush  use the offer as leverage and prep for the Senior-level switch.

You’re definitely not crazy  this is a pretty common situation right now.

With ~6 YOE, being borderline between levels is normal, and companies like Uber tend to be conservative on leveling unless system design is really strong. From your breakdown, it honestly sounds like you were close and just got pushed down by that one round.

If you’re not in a rush, taking a few more weeks to specifically improve system design (especially things like caching, pub/sub, real-time systems) could realistically help you land Senior elsewhere.

That said, if the offer has strong TC and decent WLB, accepting SDE-2 isn’t a bad move either  you can still grow into Senior quickly or re-interview later with a stronger profile.

Feels less like a capability gap and more like interview positioning + timing.

You’re not crazy — but I’d be careful here.

With ~6 YOE, you’re right on the border of Senior, and getting downleveled to SDE-2 at companies like Uber is pretty common unless you absolutely crush system design.

From what you described, your system design round (Redis deep dive) is likely what held you back which is fixable with focused prep, not a fundamental gap.

Short take:
If TC is significantly higher and WLB is decent → accepting SDE-2 isn’t a bad move
If your main goal is title + growth → you’re close enough to Senior to try again
Reality check:
Market is still a bit unpredictable → Senior roles are more selective
Internal promotion at big companies can be slow, but Uber does promote if you perform well
Upleveling externally is possible, but usually requires:
Strong system design reps
Clear “ownership” stories
My honest advice:

If you’re not in a rush, take 3–4 weeks:

Tighten system design (especially caching, pub/sub, scaling patterns)
Re-interview for Senior elsewhere

If you’re even slightly unsure about landing Senior soon →
take the offer, then re-level internally or switch again later

Key point:

You’re not far off  this isn’t a “skill gap”, it’s a positioning + interview execution gap.

If you want, I can break down exactly how to answer that Redis/pub-sub part at a Senior level  that’s probably the piece that flips your outcome next time 🔥
Interviews were virtual or you were called in office?\n\n
Were you part of hiring drive or standalone interviews? I recently completed all my rounds for SDE2 and I have 6 years of exp as well. Not sure what to do
Thanks for sharing bro. Could you also share the location?
